opportunities.rationale: Iran strait tensions keep WTI near $98; XOM benefits from higher crude realisations while UAL faces acute jet fuel cost pressure (fuel ~25% of operating costs). XOM up 5.6% since 3/13 vs UAL flat — spread is accelerating. Goldman Brent target $110 for March. Invalidated on verified ceasefire or Brent collapse below $85. Spread stop at -3%.

Second energy-vs-airlines pair diversified across different oil major (CVX) and carrier (DAL). CVX up 2.5% on the week while DAL reversed -2.4% from 3/19 highs — fuel cost fears reasserting. DAL has one of the largest un-hedged fuel exposure books among US majors. Invalidated on Hormuz de-escalation or OPEC emergency supply release. Spread stop at -3%.

Defense vs cyclical airline in an Iran war regime. LMT is the primary PAC-3 MSE air defense beneficiary ($9.8B contract, 2000 units/yr ramp); ALK is the weakest US airline, down 12.7% from 3/16 with no fuel hedging and heavy leisure-route exposure. LMT pullback from 671 to 627 offers a better entry than last week. Invalidated on ceasefire + oil normalization below $80. Spread stop at -3%.

Quality vs Reset archetype in obesity/GLP-1 space. LLY crashed 8.3% this week alongside broad market but tirzepatide pipeline fundamentals are intact; NVO continues to reprice lower on 2026 guidance cuts and Cagrisema Phase 3 miss. LLY/NVO ratio at extreme low presents mean-reversion opportunity. Downgraded from HIGH to MODERATE because LLY is falling with the market — catalyst timing is uncertain. Invalidated if LLY breaks below $870 or NVO announces positive Cagrisema update. Spread stop at -3%.

Defensive Quality vs Reset in staples. COST membership model is recession-resilient with higher-income customer base; WMT faces tariff-driven supply chain pressure and stretched 30x forward P/E. Both declining but COST down 3.6% vs WMT down 5.9% from 3/13 — COST already outperforming on a relative basis. Invalidated if WMT announces tariff mitigation plan or COST membership growth decelerates. Spread stop at -3%.

Sector Rotation archetype — energy outperforming broad index in war/oil-shock regime. XOM up 2.3% weekly while SPY down 2.1% — spread divergence is clear and accelerating. This pair captures the macro rotation without single-stock short risk. Oil above $95 is the key regime condition. Invalidated on ceasefire + oil below $85 or a strong risk-on FOMC pivot. Spread stop at -3%.
